metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test (e.g., in `test_ci_workflow_parity.py` or a new `test_makefile_actionlint_scope.py`) that reads the Makefile and asserts actionlint is NOT scoped to `RELEASE_WORKFLOW` alone — e.g., assert `RELEASE_WORKFLOW` does not appear as the sole argument to actionlint, or assert a glob/`ALL_WORKFLOWS` variable is used.
evidence: tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py L48: `"lint (ruff)": "make lint"` — only checks that `make lint` is present in both files, not what it lints
The test probes workflow text for command signatures, not Makefile content
After the change `make lint` still appears identically in both workflow files — the parity assertion passes regardless of the Makefile glob change
ABSENCE: no test in test_ci_workflow_parity.py reads or asserts anything about the Makefile's actionlint target scope
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` enforces parity of the widened `make lint` gate, but the existing parity test only checks that the string `make lint` appears in both workflows (`_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES: 'lint (ruff)': 'make lint'`). After the proposed change, `make lint` still appears unchanged in both workflows — the parity test will pass whether or not the Makefile glob was correctly widened. A future regression narrowing the glob back to `RELEASE_WORKFLOW` would not be caught by this parity test.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Confirm test.yml + gerrit-verify.yaml both run the widened make lint (parity — see tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py)'
scenarios: A follow-up PR reverts `ALL_WORKFLOWS` back to `RELEASE_WORKFLOW` in the Makefile; both workflows still say `make lint`; the parity test passes green; the regression is silent.
A new workflow file is added but the Makefile glob is mis-authored (e.g., hardcoded list missing the new file); parity test gives no signal.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a unit test that reads `Makefile`, locates the actionlint invocation line(s), and asserts that the argument is NOT `$(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)` (or equivalently, asserts that a wildcard glob or `ALL_WORKFLOWS`-style variable is used). This test would have caught the original `f2bde8d3b5` regression and will catch any future narrowing.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

evidence: file_impact is empty
findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving procedure for AC2: specify a command sequence (e.g., inject the defect into a temp workflow file, run `make lint`, assert non-zero exit code, then remove the temp file) or a named automated test that encodes this check.
evidence: No step in Scope or Acceptance Criteria describes HOW AC2 is verified (e.g., inject defect into a temp file, run make lint, assert non-zero exit, remove temp file)
The parity test reference (`tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py`) is cited only for AC4 (both legs run lint), not for AC2 (regression detection)
AC2 is the most critical criterion — it proves the bug class is actually blocked — yet it has no proving command
findings.finding: AC2 ('Reintroducing a `${{ runner.temp }}` job-level env makes `make lint` FAIL') has no described proving step — neither a concrete command sequence, a new automated test, nor a manual procedure is specified to produce evidence that the regression check actually works.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'Reintroducing a `${{ runner.temp }}` job-level env (the 8002 defect) makes `make lint` FAIL.'
scenarios: An executor widens the glob but misconfigures actionlint flags such that it silently skips context-availability checks; AC2 cannot be verified without a concrete proving procedure.
A reviewer/approver cannot confirm AC2 is met without a specified artifact or command to run.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Verified by: `echo "env:\n  BAD: \${{ runner.temp }}" >> .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml && make lint; echo $?` exits non-zero; revert the injection. Alternatively, add a pytest in `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` that writes a fixture workflow with this defect and asserts `make lint` returns non-zero.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the zizmor scope: either explicitly state 'zizmor remains scoped to release.yml (because <rationale>)' in the Scope section, or add a concrete AC for zizmor expansion — remove the ambiguous 'consider' hedge so the implementer has a clear decision.
evidence: What section: 'Expand the CI actionlint (and consider zizmor) coverage from release.yml to all .github/workflows/*.yml / *.yaml'
Scope section: lists only actionlint changes ('lint all workflows (a new ALL_WORKFLOWS/glob), not just RELEASE_WORKFLOW')
All four ACs mention only actionlint/make lint; none mention zizmor
Makefile line 103: `zizmor $(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)` — would remain scoped to release.yml unless the plan explicitly addresses it
ABSENCE: no rationale for why zizmor should not be widened alongside actionlint
findings.finding: The plan says 'consider zizmor' coverage expansion in the What section but the Scope section and all four ACs cover only actionlint, leaving zizmor's scope (stay at release.yml vs. expand to all workflows) unresolved. An implementer following the plan has no basis for deciding whether to expand zizmor, and the plan provides no rationale for the asymmetry.
findings.location: What section ('consider zizmor'); Scope section (omits zizmor); AC list (no zizmor AC)
scenarios: An implementer skips zizmor expansion (plan is silent) — a security-relevant workflow other than release.yml gets no zizmor coverage.

An implementer expands zizmor (reading 'consider' as a directive) — new suppression annotations may be needed, bloating the change and potentially causing CI failures not anticipated by the plan's 'ZERO pre-existing findings' claim.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add one sentence: 'zizmor remains scoped to release.yml only — its supply-chain / OIDC checks are release-specific (other workflows have no OIDC or publish steps) and the pinned version in [dev] has no known findings on non-release workflows.' Then remove the 'consider zizmor' parenthetical from the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope section that 'keep the macOS-leg skip logic as-is' means the conditional logic is preserved but the hard-coded 'release.yml' string in the skip notice (Makefile line 115) must be updated to reflect the widened scope (e.g. 'all .github/workflows/*.yml/*.yaml').
evidence: Makefile line 115: `echo "lint: actionlint unavailable on $$(uname -s); skipping release.yml actionlint audit (gated on the Linux CI leg)"`
Scope section: 'Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is' — this phrase may be read as preserving the literal string, not just the logic
findings.finding: The Makefile's macOS-skip notice (line 115) hard-codes the string 'release.yml' in a user-visible message. The plan instructs 'Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is' but the 'as-is' content includes this literal string — after widening to all workflows the message will misrepresent the actual scope being audited, potentially misleading developers about what was skipped.
findings.location: Makefile line 115; Scope section ('Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is')
scenarios: On a macOS dev machine without actionlint, the notice prints 'skipping release.yml actionlint audit' even though the widened gate covers all 15 workflows — a developer reading this could incorrectly conclude only release.yml was intended to be checked.
findings.suggested_fix: Update the Scope section to: 'Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is, updating the user-facing notice string to reference all workflows rather than release.yml.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that verifies the macOS-leg actionlint skip and pinned-binary install behavior is preserved after the Makefile lint target is widened.
evidence: Scope: 'Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is.'
ABSENCE: No AC reads 'make lint on macOS still skips / installs the pinned binary correctly' or equivalent.
AC1–AC4 collectively cover: glob expansion, defect regression, zero findings, dual-leg — none verify the macOS skip is untouched.
findings.finding: The Scope section states 'Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is' as an explicit constraint, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that this existing behavior is preserved after the change.
findings.location: Scope section, bullet 2: 'Keep the macOS-leg skip / pinned-binary install logic as-is.'
scenarios: An implementer rewrites the Makefile lint target to use a glob and inadvertently removes or breaks the macOS-skip or pinned-binary install; all four ACs pass (they run on Linux CI) while the macOS leg silently breaks.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] On macOS, `make lint` either skips actionlint (existing skip logic) or invokes the pinned binary as before; the macOS-skip/install path is not removed by the glob expansion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document how the '0 pre-existing actionlint findings across all workflows' claim was verified (e.g., add the command and its output as a plan appendix, or add a CI dry-run step), so reviewers can confirm the 'no allowlist needed' premise before the gate is widened.
evidence: Cannot execute actionlint against the repo to verify the '0 findings' claim
.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml L372: `--basetemp="${{ runner.temp }}/rebar-basetemp"` (step-level, valid)
.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml L385: `--basetemp="${{ runner.temp }}/rebar-basetemp-int"` (step-level, valid)
.github/workflows/test.yml L391, L404, L456: similar step-level runner.temp uses
docs/identity-setup.md:93: `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY: ${{ runner.temp }}/signing-key` — in a doc, not a workflow file
The claim is the sole justification for 'no allowlist or cleanup is needed'
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml exits 0 with 0 findings after the 8002 fix' as a premise for claiming no allowlist or cleanup is needed. However, `gerrit-verify.yaml` contains `--basetemp="${{ runner.temp }}/rebar-basetemp"` inside `run:` steps and `docs/identity-setup.md` shows `${{ runner.temp }}` in a signing-key path. More critically, `docs/identity-setup.md:93` contains `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY: ${{ runner.temp }}/signing-key` as a YAML snippet — actionlint may or may not flag step-level `runner.temp` uses in the actual workflow files. The '0 findings' claim cannot be verified by repo-file tools alone and is the load-bearing justification for 'no allowlist or cleanup is needed'.
findings.location: What section: 'Measured cost is ZERO today: actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml exits 0 with 0 findings after the 8002 fix, so no allowlist or cleanup is needed to turn the gate on.'
scenarios: Some of the 14 non-release.yml workflows contain actionlint findings (e.g., for other context-availability issues) that would need an allowlist; the plan ships the widened gate and immediately breaks CI.
A workflow uses a non-standard shell construct that actionlint flags; the 'zero cost' premise is wrong and the gate breaks the build before the fix lands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/releasing.md` (line ~129) as an explicit edit target in the plan's Scope, updating the 'scoped to `release.yml`' phrasing to reflect all-workflows coverage.
evidence: docs/releasing.md:129: 'enforced by **zizmor + actionlint scoped to `release.yml`** under `make lint`'
Plan Scope section does not list docs/releasing.md as an edit target.
After the change, actionlint will cover all workflows, making this statement incorrect.
findings.finding: `docs/releasing.md:129` explicitly states actionlint is 'scoped to `release.yml`' under `make lint` — this will be a false, stale statement after the change and is not enumerated as an edit target in the plan.
scenarios: A developer reads docs/releasing.md and believes only release.yml is validated, causing confusion or manual re-scoping of actionlint invocations.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the sentence to: 'enforced by **zizmor** (scoped to `release.yml`) + **actionlint** (all `.github/workflows/*.yml` / `*.yaml`) under `make lint`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the Makefile comment updates (lines 19, 97, 107, 115) in the plan's Scope so the stale 'scoped to release.yml' / 'release.yml only' wording is updated alongside the functional change.
evidence: Makefile:19: '# to release.yml under `make lint`'
Makefile:97: 'scoped zizmor/actionlint on release.yml. The gate CI runs.'
Makefile:107: '# matrix leg), skip it with a notice: release.yml only runs on ubuntu'
Makefile:115: 'echo "lint: actionlint unavailable on ...; skipping release.yml actionlint audit"'
Plan Scope section only mentions adding 'ALL_WORKFLOWS/glob' without noting comment updates.
findings.finding: Several Makefile inline comments that say 'scoped to release.yml' / 'release.yml only' (lines 19, 97, 107, 115) will become stale after the change, but the plan does not enumerate updating them — leaving misleading in-file documentation.
scenarios: A developer reading the Makefile after the change sees comments still saying 'release.yml only' and either undoes the widening thinking it was a mistake, or is confused about which files are actually linted.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify zizmor's scope: either add an explicit AC for it or add an 'out of scope for this task' note in the plan to prevent executor uncertainty.
evidence: 'consider zizmor' — no corresponding scope bullet, AC, or explicit out-of-scope declaration
Scope section and all four ACs reference only actionlint, never zizmor
An executor cannot determine whether this is deferred, optional, or simply dropped
findings.finding: 'consider zizmor' appears in the What section as an unresolved option but is absent from Scope and all ACs, leaving the executor uncertain whether adding zizmor is in scope for this task.
findings.location: What section: 'Expand the CI actionlint (and consider zizmor) coverage...'
scenarios: An executor implements only actionlint widening and ships; reviewer asks 'what about zizmor?' — scope was unclear.
An executor spends time researching/implementing zizmor when it was never intended to be in this task.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Scope section: 'zizmor evaluation is explicitly out of scope for this task and tracked separately.' Alternatively, add an AC if zizmor integration is intended.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` is a **new deliverable** this plan must create (and add it to Scope/ACs explicitly) or a mistaken reference — the file does not currently exist in the repo.
evidence: Search for 'test_ci_workflow_parity' across tests/ returns no matches.
Search for 'workflow_parity' across the whole repo returns no matches in tests/.
The plan uses this file reference as a factual grounding for the parity requirement, implying it currently enforces the gate — but it does not exist.
findings.finding: The Scope section cites 'tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py' as an existing file that verifies parity between test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml, but no such file exists in the repository. This is a false capability assertion embedded in a load-bearing plan constraint.
findings.location: Scope bullet: 'Confirm test.yml + gerrit-verify.yaml both run the widened make lint (parity — see tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py)'
scenarios: An implementor follows the plan, trusts that parity is already tested by test_ci_workflow_parity.py, and skips creating it — leaving the pre-merge vs post-merge parity assertion un-tested.
A reviewer accepts the Scope bullet as fully covered when it is actually an open deliverable, not an existing safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical '(parity — see tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py)' with either: (a) '(parity — create tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py to assert both workflows invoke `make lint` over all workflows)' if a new test is intended, or (b) a reference to the actual existing parity mechanism if one exists elsewhere.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact actionlint multi-file/glob invocation syntax against `actionlint --help` (or the rhysd/actionlint docs), and specify whether `ALL_WORKFLOWS` will use Make's `$(wildcard ...)` function or a shell-level glob in the recipe — document the chosen form in the Makefile comment so it is auditable.
evidence: Partially-verified: Makefile already invokes `actionlint $(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)` (single-file arg), proving the tool and its file-arg invocation mode exist.
Unverified: multi-file glob invocation (`actionlint *.yml *.yaml`) not confirmed against `actionlint --help` or docs in this review.

Makefile uses a shell subshell pattern (`$$al ... $(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)`) where glob expansion depends on the shell executing the recipe line — a Make variable expansion is not the same as a literal shell glob, and the plan does not specify whether `ALL_WORKFLOWS` will be a Make glob (using `wildcard`) or a literal shell glob in the recipe.
EVIDENCE CLASS: Partially-verified (tool exists and is used; specific multi-file/glob surface unconfirmed)
findings.finding: The plan asserts `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` as the widened invocation but does not confirm the multi-file/glob flag-level syntax against `actionlint --help` or the tool's documented CLI surface. The existing Makefile uses `actionlint <single-file>` (via a Make variable), and while multi-file invocation is standard for this tool, the exact behavior with shell globs passed as separate arguments versus a directory argument (e.g. `actionlint .github/workflows/`) is Partially-verified, not Verified. A silent no-op (e.g., glob unexpanded in the Make recipe's shell subshell context) would defeat the gate without failing loudly.
findings.location: What section: 'actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml exits 0 with 0 findings'; Scope: 'Makefile lint target: lint all workflows (a new ALL_WORKFLOWS/glob)'
scenarios: If the Makefile uses a literal shell glob in the `$$al` invocation string (e.g., `"$$al" .github/workflows/*.yml`) but the shell does not expand it (e.g., because it is inside a double-quoted string or the glob is in a Make variable that does not use `$(wildcard ...)`), actionlint receives a literal unexpanded glob string, which it may treat as a non-existent file — silently passing or erroring in a confusing way.
If actionlint requires a directory argument rather than separate file globs for bulk linting, the proposed invocation form may behave differently than expected.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `$(wildcard .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml)` as a Make variable (evaluated at Make parse time, guaranteed to expand) rather than a shell glob in the recipe. E.g.: `ALL_WORKFLOWS := $(wildcard .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml)` and then `"$$al" $(ALL_WORKFLOWS)` in the lint recipe. This is unambiguous and testable.
